title: AFP Chief Confirms Balikatan 2026 Starts April 20 — Biggest Ever, Japan Sends Combat Troops for First Time
detail: AFP Chief of Staff Gen. Romeo Brawner Jr. confirmed on April 1 that Balikatan 2026 — the annual Philippines-US joint military exercise — would commence around April 20, 2026 and would be the 'biggest ever' in the exercise's history, despite the ongoing US-Iran conflict in the Middle East. Most significantly, Japan will send combat-capable Self-Defense Forces units to participate directly in training scenarios for the first time since World War II, with Japanese troops carrying weapons during joint exercises — a historic milestone in Japan's post-war security posture. Australia and France will also join as full partners. The exercise focus areas include multi-domain operations, coastal defense, anti-ship tactics, missile systems, and island area denial — a clear deterrence signal directed at Chinese military planners. Pre-exercise activities, including US-Philippine troop engagements in Palawan, were already underway as of March 28. Over 500 US-Philippines joint security engagements were approved for 2026 under bilateral planning guidance agreed at the August 2025 Mutual Defense Board–Security Engagement Board (MDB-SEB) meeting in Hawaii.

media.caption: Balikatan 2026 confirmed as biggest ever with Japan sending combat troops for first time

title: Three Chinese Coast Guard Cutters Bracket Philippines' Northernmost Batanes Islands Near Bashi Channel
detail: Three Chinese coast guard cutters, each over 330 feet in length, were tracked by Philippine and US maritime surveillance assets 'bracketing' the Philippines' northernmost Batanes Islands province near the Bashi Channel — the strategically critical waterway between Luzon and Taiwan. Maritime analyst Ray Powell described the deployment as 'a new level of aggression,' noting that Chinese coast guard vessels had not previously operated in such numbers this far north in Philippine waters. Batanes is closer to southern Taiwan than to Manila. The deployment coincided with the broader South China Sea tension cycle following the Quanzhou talks and BRP Benguet near-collision, and came amid Balikatan 2026 preparations involving NMESIS anti-ship missile deployments to the Batanes Islands. The Philippines indicated it may pursue expanded joint patrols with the US if Chinese harassment in northern Philippine waters continues.
